is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum dhfr alleles from Kilifi, Kenya (title and throughout).

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports dhfr codons 51, 59, 108 (target markers: Pfdhfr 51,59,108). Table 2 gives amino acids at dhfr codons 51, 59, 108.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 2 gives 'Amino acids at dhfr codons 51, 59, and 108, no. (%)' per year (NCS, ICN, NRN, IRN with counts and percentages), and No. with data/total studied per period.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples collected in Kilifi District/Kilifi District Hospital, Kenya — a named sub-national coastal town, below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Table 2 breaks down frequencies per single year (1987, 1988, 1993, 1994, 1995, 1997...2006), each within 3 years, even though the overall span is 1987-2006.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human field/clinical isolates (dried blood spots, whole blood) collected in Kilifi District Hospital, Kenya.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study genotyping field isolates; reports original genotype/haplotype data.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples were reused from prior studies: 'All samples used in the current study were collected previously for other purposes' — dried blood spots (1987-88 [21]), frozen whole blood (1993-95 [22,23]), filter paper (1997-2006 Kilifi District Hospital). These original collections were published separately.

supplement.evidence: Extractable per-year, per-codon frequencies are in Table 2 of the main paper; supplementary tables contain primers and full microsatellite haplotype data but are not needed for dhfr codon frequency extraction.

assessment.summary: Primary study genotyping P. falciparum dhfr codons 51, 59, 108 in field isolates from Kilifi District, Kenya collected 1987-2006. Table 2 provides per-year extractable frequencies (counts and percentages) of amino acid combinations at these codons, satisfying marker, frequency, sub-country, and temporal resolution criteria. Samples were reused from previously published collections, giving a medium duplicate risk.
